introSection: Nemesis Reborn is a steel inverted terrain roller coaster located at Alton Towers in Alton, Staffordshire, England, UK. Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it opened in 1994 under the name Nemesis and was the first inverted roller coaster outside of North America. It is also the first Bolliger & Mabillard coaster outside of North America.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
There were originally two other plans for Nemesis (called Secret Weapon 1 and Secret Weapon 2). The first was scrapped due to height restrictions and the second was an Arrow Dynamics pipeline roller coaster. John Wardley, design consultant of Nemesis, rode a prototype but found it to have a lack of energy. This is when Nemesis, or Secret Weapon 3 as it was then known, was designed.

Nemesis opened on 19 March 1994.

The opening in 1994 of The Big One, Nemesis and Shockwave at Drayton Manor resulted in 1994 being celebrated by United Kingdom's parks and fans as the "Year of the Rollercoaster".



In January 2022, Alton Towers submitted plans for renovation works to the local council. This included replacement of the track from the top of the lift hill to the start of the brake run and the replacement of the majority of supports. On 21 September 2022, the park announced that Nemesis would close on 6 November with social media posts reading "By order of the Phalanx, Nemesis is closing. You have until November 6 to ride. Please make your way to Alton Towers Resort in an orderly fashion."

On 8 January 2024, the park announced that Nemesis would be renamed Nemesis Reborn.

On 20 January 2024, Nemesis Reborn started testing and a video was released of the testing by Alton Towers. This showed the train traversing the track with an updated soundtrack in the background.

On 21 February 2024, Alton Towers announced the ride will be reopening on 16 March.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Nemesis Reborn features four inversions which twist around the ground, trees and the "river of blood." The entire ride is built partially into the ground to keep it below the tree line. Nemesis Reborn has a pre-drop, a feature employed by the ride manufacturer to reduce strain on the lift hill.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: When the ride first opened, Nemesis's track was painted an off-cream color and its supports were painted black. When it was repainted in 2016, the track became white with a rust effect. With the ride's recent renovation, the track was replaced, including black track with a red vein design, and black supports.

sections.title: Related rides
sections.content: A sister roller coaster, Nemesis Inferno, was built in 2003 at Thorpe Park. Another ride, Nemesis: Sub Terra, was built in 2012. It is an ABC Rides indoor drop tower themed around a military compound owned by the Phalanx, the organization that captured the Nemesis creature. Many special effects are used, with the drop tower being only a small part of the entire experience.

fullText:
Nemesis Reborn is a steel inverted terrain roller coaster located at Alton Towers in Alton, Staffordshire, England, UK. Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it opened in 1994 under the name Nemesis and was the first inverted roller coaster outside of North America. It is also the first Bolliger & Mabillard coaster outside of North America.

There were originally two other plans for Nemesis (called Secret Weapon 1 and Secret Weapon 2). The first was scrapped due to height restrictions and the second was an Arrow Dynamics pipeline roller coaster. John Wardley, design consultant of Nemesis, rode a prototype but found it to have a lack of energy. This is when Nemesis, or Secret Weapon 3 as it was then known, was designed.

Nemesis opened on 19 March 1994.

The opening in 1994 of The Big One, Nemesis and Shockwave at Drayton Manor resulted in 1994 being celebrated by United Kingdom's parks and fans as the "Year of the Rollercoaster".



In January 2022, Alton Towers submitted plans for renovation works to the local council. This included replacement of the track from the top of the lift hill to the start of the brake run and the replacement of the majority of supports. On 21 September 2022, the park announced that Nemesis would close on 6 November with social media posts reading "By order of the Phalanx, Nemesis is closing. You have until November 6 to ride. Please make your way to Alton Towers Resort in an orderly fashion."

On 8 January 2024, the park announced that Nemesis would be renamed Nemesis Reborn.

On 20 January 2024, Nemesis Reborn started testing and a video was released of the testing by Alton Towers. This showed the train traversing the track with an updated soundtrack in the background.

On 21 February 2024, Alton Towers announced the ride will be reopening on 16 March.

Nemesis Reborn features four inversions which twist around the ground, trees and the "river of blood." The entire ride is built partially into the ground to keep it below the tree line. Nemesis Reborn has a pre-drop, a feature employed by the ride manufacturer to reduce strain on the lift hill.

When the ride first opened, Nemesis's track was painted an off-cream color and its supports were painted black. When it was repainted in 2016, the track became white with a rust effect. With the ride's recent renovation, the track was replaced, including black track with a red vein design, and black supports.



A sister roller coaster, Nemesis Inferno, was built in 2003 at Thorpe Park. Another ride, Nemesis: Sub Terra, was built in 2012. It is an ABC Rides indoor drop tower themed around a military compound owned by the Phalanx, the organization that captured the Nemesis creature. Many special effects are used, with the drop tower being only a small part of the entire experience.
